connectors.name: AlienVault OTX - threat pulses (CTI)
connectors.description: Pulls threat 'pulses' from AlienVault OTX / LevelBlue Open Threat Exchange and maps each to a normalized XORCISM intel item -> XTHREAT.INTELEXCHANGE (the threat graph + ATT&CK coverage heatmap). Each pulse carries indicators (IPs, domains, hostnames, URLs, file hashes, CVEs), MITRE ATT&CK technique ids, adversary, malware families, targeted industries/countries and tags. The named CTI source XORCISM was missing alongside MISP / OpenCTI / abuse.ch / VirusTotal / GreyNoise / Shodan. Modes: live (your subscribed pulses via the OTX API key), file (an exported pulses JSON), or demo. Read-only. Config: OTX_API_KEY (or pass api_key).

parameters.name: api_key
parameters.help: OTX API key (overrides OTX_API_KEY) for live /pulses/subscribed

parameters.name: limit
parameters.help: Max pulses to pull (live)

parameters.name: modified_since
parameters.help: ISO timestamp - only pulses modified since (live; default: all subscribed)

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path to an exported OTX pulses JSON ({"results":[...]} or an array). A demo sample is used if omitted and no API key is set.

connectors.name: Amass
connectors.description: Imports attack-surface / subdomain enumeration results from OWASP Amass (https://github.com/owasp-amass/amass). Each discovered name becomes an ASSET (hostname + resolved IP). Offline mode: parse an `amass enum -json` output file (JSON lines or array). Live mode: if `domain` is set and the `amass` binary is on the worker PATH, runs `amass enum -d <domain> -json`. No DB access in run.py (worker-safe). Run live only within an authorised engagement scope.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to an `amass enum -json` output file (JSON lines or a JSON array) to import.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.help: Live mode: domain to enumerate with the local `amass` binary (amass enum -d <domain> -json).

connectors.name: Artemis
connectors.description: Imports web vulnerability findings from Artemis (CERT Polska, https://github.com/CERT-Polska/Artemis), a modular scanner that checks websites for misconfigurations and known vulnerabilities (exposed .git/VCS, outdated CMS like Joomla/WordPress, directory listing, subdomain takeover, vulnerable services, Nuclei templates, weak passwords…). Each scanned target host/URL becomes an ASSET; each interesting finding (module + message, CVE if referenced) becomes a VULNERABILITY with a mapped severity. Offline mode: parse an Artemis results/report JSON export. Live mode: with `target` (+ `api_base`), submit a scan to the Artemis API and import the results. Active/intrusive web scanning. No DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to an Artemis results/report JSON export to import (offline mode).

parameters.name: target
parameters.help: Live mode: host or URL for Artemis to scan (must be in the engagement scope).

parameters.name: api_base
parameters.help: Live mode: base URL of the Artemis web API (default http://localhost:5000).

parameters.name: api_key
parameters.help: Live mode: API token for the Artemis backend (env ARTEMIS_API_TOKEN also honored).

connectors.name: Atomic Red Team — BAS / AEV runner
connectors.description: Runs Atomic Red Team (redcanaryco) atomic tests on the (remote) worker host and reports the per-test outcome to XORCISM's emulation layer (EMULATIONRUN / EMULATIONRESULT), powering the ATT&CK validation-coverage heatmap — the 'validate' stage of CTEM / Adversarial Exposure Validation. Wraps the canonical Invoke-AtomicTest executor. SAFE by default: execute=false only PLANS/checks the technique (lists atomics, optional -CheckPrereqs) without running anything; execute=true actually runs it (authorized BAS only — the runner enforces engagement scope). Offline: pass `file` = a saved result export; no config = a built-in simulated sample.

parameters.name: technique
parameters.help: ATT&CK technique to run, e.g. T1059.001 (uses Invoke-AtomicTest)

parameters.name: target
parameters.help: Host the atomic runs ON (the worker's own host). REQUIRED to execute — it must be inside the engagement scope (ROE), so execution is authorized + audited. Leave empty for plan/check only.

parameters.name: test_guids
parameters.help: Optional comma-separated atomic GUIDs to limit to specific tests

parameters.name: execute
parameters.help: false = plan/check only (no execution); true = actually run the atomics. Requires `target` in an active engagement scope.

parameters.name: check_prereqs
parameters.help: In plan mode, also run -CheckPrereqs

parameters.name: cleanup
parameters.help: After execution, run the atomic's -Cleanup

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Offline: a saved atomic-result export to import instead of running

connectors.name: AuditBoard
connectors.description: AuditBoard — connected risk, audit & compliance platform. Imports AuditBoard policies + evidence/documents into the XORCISM controlled-document register (XCOMPLIANCE.DOCUMENT). Offline: a JSON export. Live: set AUDITBOARD_BASE (https://<tenant>.auditboardapp.com) + AUDITBOARD_API_TOKEN (Bearer). Tool: https://www.auditboard.com.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Offline JSON export of AuditBoard policies/evidence.

parameters.name: base
parameters.help: AuditBoard tenant base URL (https://<tenant>.auditboardapp.com). Overrides AUDITBOARD_BASE. Live needs AUDITBOARD_API_TOKEN.

parameters.name: max_items
parameters.help: Maximum documents to import.

connectors.name: burpwn
connectors.description: Imports captured web traffic from burpwn (own2pwn-fr/burpwn) — a standalone Rust intercepting proxy + execution sandbox for agentic web pentesting that stores every HTTP/HTTPS flow in a per-session SQLite database. Offline mode parses a burpwn flow export (JSON: `burpwn req list --json`); live mode (Linux, requires bubblewrap/nftables) runs a command through the proxy via `burpwn exec -- <cmd>` and reads the captured flows. The target host becomes an ASSET, each discovered endpoint becomes a component on it, and interesting observations (auth endpoints, server errors, reflected/sensitive parameters) become VULNERABILITY findings. Active web pentest (intrusive). Wired into the web-app pentest attack chains.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a burpwn flow export to import (JSON, e.g. `burpwn req list --json > flows.json`).

parameters.name: target
parameters.help: Live mode (Linux): base URL to exercise through the burpwn proxy (must be in the engagement scope).

parameters.name: command
parameters.help: Live mode: command run inside the burpwn sandbox ({target} is substituted).

connectors.name: MITRE Caldera — adversary-emulation automation
connectors.description: Automates adversary emulation against a remote MITRE Caldera server (v2 API). action=import (default, READ-ONLY) reads existing operations: agents → ASSET, executed abilities → findings AND → emulation_results (EMULATIONRUN/EMULATIONRESULT → the ATT&CK validation-coverage heatmap). action=launch creates + starts an operation (adversary vs. an agent group) on the remote host — automated offensive emulation (admin-only; blast radius = the Caldera agent group you deployed), optionally `wait` then import its results. Target any remote Caldera host via caldera_url; schedulable (XSCHEDULE) for recurring automation. Key stays in the worker env. Config: CALDERA_URL + CALDERA_API_KEY.

parameters.help: import = read operations (read-only); launch = create + start an operation on the remote host

parameters.name: group

connectors.name: Censys
connectors.description: Imports internet/attack-surface data from Censys (https://search.censys.io). Each host becomes an ASSET (IP, OS), each exposed service a SERVICE (host:port), and detected software CPEs are linked to the asset. Offline mode: parse a Censys hosts export / API JSON (a list of hosts, or a v2 hosts-search `{result:{hits:[...]}}` response). Live mode: if `query` is set and CENSYS_API_ID / CENSYS_API_SECRET are in the worker env, calls the v2 hosts search API (needs `pip install requests`). Read-only OSINT (non-intrusive). No DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a Censys hosts JSON (export or v2 hosts-search response) to import.

parameters.name: query
parameters.help: Live mode: Censys hosts search query (e.g. `services.service_name: HTTP and location.country: FR`). Needs CENSYS_API_ID/SECRET in the worker env.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.help: Live mode: maximum number of hosts to import.

connectors.name: Chainguard
connectors.description: Imports container-image vulnerability results from Chainguard (https://www.chainguard.dev) — minimal, low-CVE Wolfi-based images and the chainctl platform. The scanned image becomes an ASSET, each package a component, and each vulnerability a VULNERABILITY / ASSETVULNERABILITY (ref = CVE/GHSA, severity). Offline mode: import a Grype JSON report (`grype <image> -o json`, the scanner Chainguard tooling uses), an `{images:[{name,vulnerabilities:[...]}]}` export, or a flat findings list. Live mode: if `image` is set and the `chainctl` or `grype` binary is on the worker, scans it. Optional env CHAINGUARD_TOKEN for chainctl auth. No DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

parameters.help: Path on the worker to a Chainguard/Grype JSON vulnerability report to import.

connectors.name: Slack / Mattermost — outbound notifications
connectors.description: Outbound (action) connector: posts a XORCISM alert / incident / message to a Slack or Mattermost channel via an incoming webhook (both share the same payload format). Use it to push SOC alerts, exposure findings or chain results to your team channel — the SOAR/ChatOps complement to the inbound SOC-tool connectors. Config (worker environment variables): SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL or MATTERMOST_WEBHOOK_URL (or pass `webhook`). Severity colours the message; with no webhook (or dry_run) it returns the built payload without sending. Does NOT read or write the database.

parameters.name: text
parameters.help: Message body (Markdown). Required unless title is given.

parameters.name: title
parameters.help: Optional bold header / alert name

parameters.name: severity
parameters.help: critical | high | medium | low | info — colours the message

parameters.name: link
parameters.help: Optional URL (e.g. deep-link to the alert in XORCISM)

parameters.name: channel
parameters.help: Optional channel override (Slack/Mattermost webhooks that allow it)

parameters.name: username
parameters.help: Display name for the bot message

parameters.name: webhook
parameters.help: Incoming-webhook URL (overrides the env var)

parameters.name: dry_run
parameters.help: Build the payload but do not send it (returns the payload)

connectors.name: CheckCle

Parses an exported JSON, or pulls live from CheckCle's PocketBase API — CHECKCLE_URL + CHECKCLE_TOKEN, or CHECKCLE_USER + CHECKCLE_PASSWORD (PocketBase auth).

connectors.name: GCVE CPE Editor - curated CPE dictionary
connectors.description: Pulls curated CPE (Common Platform Enumeration) records from a GCVE-EU CPE Editor instance (https://github.com/gcve-eu/cpe-editor, v1.1.0+) via its read-only OpenAPI (vendors / products / cpes / changes) and normalizes each to a CPE dictionary entry -> XORCISM.CPE (the platform/software identifiers used for CVE<->asset matching, SCA and OVAL/vulnerable-configuration tests). CPE Editor makes curated CPE data easier to explore, review, publish and run in production, with alias/duplicate detection and NVD CPE-Match / PURL mappings. Modes: live (base_url -> GET /api/v1/cpes), file (an exported JSON or NDJSON dataset from the editor), or demo. Read-only. Config: CPE_EDITOR_URL (or pass base_url), optional CPE_EDITOR_TOKEN bearer.

connectors.name: CyberArk
connectors.description: CyberArk Privileged Access Management (Self-Hosted PVWA / Privilege Cloud) identity sync via the REST API. Collects privileged accounts (→ non-human/privileged identities, with their target address, platform, safe and secret type) and vault users (→ human or component identities) into XORCISM (IDENTITY), so privileged credentials feed the Identity & IAM inventory for orphaned-NHI, stale-secret (rotation) and privilege detection. Authenticates with a service account — secrets from the worker environment: CYBERARK_BASE_URL, CYBERARK_USERNAME, CYBERARK_PASSWORD (and optional CYBERARK_AUTH_METHOD = CyberArk | LDAP | RADIUS | Windows). REST API: https://docs.cyberark.com/pam-self-hosted/latest/en/content/webservices/implementing%20privileged%20account%20security%20web%20services%20.htm.

connectors.name: DARKWATCH-OSINT — dark-web asset monitoring
connectors.description: Imports dark-web monitoring alerts from DARKWATCH-OSINT (defensive .onion crawling / digital-risk-protection platform by K3lgy, https://github.com/K3lgy/DARKWATCH-OSINT). Monitored assets that surface in data leaks, ransomware posts or exploit sales become ASSETs, and each alert becomes a finding (data-leak / ransomware / exploit exposure). Extracted entities (domains, IPs, emails, file hashes) are mapped to assets where possible. Read-only import (non-intrusive): DARKWATCH-OSINT itself runs on YOUR infrastructure (Tor + crawler + Elasticsearch); this connector only ingests its output. Config via worker environment variables (NEVER in the UI): DARKWATCH_API_URL (optional, e.g. http://127.0.0.1:8080), DARKWATCH_API_KEY (optional), DARKWATCH_VERIFY_TLS (0 to skip TLS verification). Offline mode: supply a saved alerts/entities JSON export through the 'file' parameter. The API mode needs `pip install requests` on the worker.

parameters.name: brand
parameters.help: Organisation / brand / asset under monitoring (e.g. example.com). Labels the findings and is the fallback asset for alerts with no extractable host.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a DARKWATCH-OSINT export (JSON: alerts and/or monitored entities). If omitted, DARKWATCH_API_URL is queried.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.help: Maximum number of alerts/entities to import.

connectors.name: depx — malicious package / supply-chain audit
connectors.description: Audits local lockfiles and SBOMs for KNOWN MALICIOUS packages (supply-chain compromise) with ProjectDiscovery depx, cross-referencing the OpenSSF Malicious Packages dataset and a live intelligence feed (hijacked publishes, credential stealers, install-script backdoors, typosquats). Imports the audited project as an ASSET and each malicious-package advisory as a finding (VULNERABILITY / ASSETVULNERABILITY); affected packages are recorded as components/CPEs. Non-intrusive: local read of dependency files + advisory lookups. Requires no secrets/env vars. Install the binary on the worker: curl -sfL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/projectdiscovery/depx/main/scripts/install.sh | sh

parameters.name: source
parameters.help: Path on the worker to audit: a project directory, a lockfile (package-lock.json, yarn.lock, requirements.txt, go.sum, Gemfile.lock…) or an SBOM (CycloneDX/SPDX).

parameters.name: project
parameters.help: Application/asset name to attach the findings to (optional; default « depx audit »).

connectors.name: detections.ai — Intel Exchange (threat intel)
connectors.description: Gathers community threat-intelligence reports from the detections.ai Intel Exchange (https://detections.ai/intel-exchange) using a REAL headless browser (Playwright/Chromium), capturing the JSON its single-page app loads from the BFF API (/bff/api/v1/intel). Each report (title, summary, author, date, MITRE ATT&CK techniques, adversary groups, malware/tools, CVE references, views) is imported into XTHREAT.INTELEXCHANGE — idempotent by source URL — and its ATT&CK techniques are cross-linked into INTELEXCHANGEATTACK so they appear in the ATT&CK matrix coverage. Read-only, non-intrusive, no authentication. Worker setup: pip install playwright && playwright install chromium. Offline/air-gapped import is possible via the 'file' parameter (a saved BFF JSON payload).

parameters.name: max_items
parameters.help: Maximum number of intel-exchange items to import (most recent first).

parameters.name: headful
parameters.help: Run the browser with a visible window (debugging). Default: headless.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Offline mode: import from a saved JSON payload (the BFF /bff/api/v1/intel response, or a JSON list of items) instead of scraping. Mainly for testing / air-gapped use.

connectors.name: DrogonSec — SAST/SCA/Secrets/IaC (SARIF)
connectors.description: Runs DrogonSec — an all-in-one open-source scanner combining SAST (20+ languages), SCA (dependency vulnerabilities), secret detection (50+ patterns) and Infrastructure-as-Code analysis — on a local source path or git repository, and imports the results via OASIS SARIF (shared SARIF parser). The scanned project becomes an ASSET; each finding becomes a finding (VULNERABILITY / ASSETVULNERABILITY) with severity from the SARIF rule level / security-severity, and the run is recorded as a DevSecOps SAST scan. Non-intrusive local code scan; no source or secrets leave the worker. Install on the worker: go install github.com/filipi86/drogonsec/cmd/drogonsec@latest (or use the drogonsec binary on PATH / the Docker image).

parameters.name: source
parameters.help: Path on the worker to scan (a directory or a git repository).

parameters.name: project
parameters.help: Application/asset name to attach the findings to (default: derived from the source / SARIF).

connectors.name: EMAIL-CRAWL — OSINT email harvesting
connectors.description: Imports the email addresses harvested from a target website by EMAIL-CRAWL (an OSINT web crawler that extracts emails from contact/team pages, mailtos, etc.). For an attack-surface view those exposed addresses are phishing / social-engineering targets, so the crawled domain becomes an ASSET and each email becomes an informational finding on it (noting the source page and whether it is an on-domain corporate address). Offline (non-intrusive): pass `file` = an EmailCrawl JSON export. Live (ACTIVE OSINT RECON — only with authorization): set EMAILCRAWL_BIN (path to EmailCrawl.py on the worker) and pass `target` (the site to crawl) + optional `maxPages`. No config / no file → the bundled sample.

parameters.name: target
parameters.help: URL/domain to crawl (live mode; requires EMAILCRAWL_BIN on the worker). Active recon — authorized targets only.

parameters.name: maxPages
parameters.help: Max pages to crawl in live mode (EmailCrawl --max-pages; default 200).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Offline EmailCrawl JSON export ({emails:[...]} / {results:[{email, source_url}]} / a flat list).

connectors.name: Microsoft Entra ID - Conditional Access policies (Zero Trust policy register)
connectors.description: Inbound connector: ingests Microsoft Entra ID Conditional Access policies into XCOMPLIANCE.ZTPOLICY - the Zero Trust policy register (NIST SP 800-207 Policy Engine view) behind the /zero-trust Automation & Governance pillars. Normalizes each policy to subject x resource x conditions x grant controls (require MFA / compliant device / block) and state (enabled | disabled | report-only). Modes: `file` (an exported policies JSON - a Graph value[] array), `live` (Microsoft Graph /identity/conditionalAccess/policies with a token), or `demo` (a built-in sample). Read-only against Entra. Config: GRAPH_TOKEN (or pass `token`). Idempotent by policy id.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path to an exported Conditional Access policies JSON (a Graph {"value":[...]} object or array). A demo sample is used if omitted and no token is set.

parameters.name: token
parameters.help: Microsoft Graph bearer token (overrides GRAPH_TOKEN) for live /identity/conditionalAccess/policies

connectors.name: Microsoft Entra ID
connectors.description: Microsoft 365 / Entra ID (Azure AD) identity & device sync via the Microsoft Graph API. Collects and synchronizes users (human identities), service principals & managed identities (non-human identities / NHI) and registered devices (assets) into XORCISM (IDENTITY + ASSET). App-only auth (OAuth2 client credentials) — secrets from the worker environment: ENTRA_TENANT_ID, ENTRA_CLIENT_ID, ENTRA_CLIENT_SECRET. Requires the application permissions User.Read.All, Application.Read.All and Device.Read.All. Microsoft Graph: https://learn.microsoft.com/graph/overview.

parameters.name: tenant
parameters.help: Entra tenant id (GUID) or primary domain. Overrides ENTRA_TENANT_ID.

parameters.name: include
parameters.help: Comma-separated object types to sync: users, servicePrincipals, devices (default: all three).

parameters.name: max_items
parameters.help: Maximum objects to pull per type (default 1000).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Offline mode: a saved Microsoft Graph JSON export to parse instead of calling the API. Accepts {users:[],servicePrincipals:[],devices:[]} or raw Graph {value:[...]} arrays.

connectors.name: Microsoft Entra ID - sign-in logs (UEBA / Zero Trust)
connectors.description: Inbound connector: ingests Microsoft Entra ID sign-in logs into XORCISM.IDENTITYSIGNIN - the continuous-verification telemetry behind the /zero-trust Identity pillar and the UEBA session-risk (zerotrust.ts sessionRisk: MFA-less sign-ins, failed bursts, impossible travel, Entra risk level). Normalizes user, time, IP, location, device, MFA, result and risk. Modes: `file` (an exported sign-in JSON - a Graph value[] array), `live` (Microsoft Graph /auditLogs/signIns with a token), or `demo` (a built-in sample). Read-only against Entra. Config: GRAPH_TOKEN (or pass `token`). Idempotent by Entra sign-in id.

connectors.name: EU Vulnerability Database (EUVD)
connectors.description: Imports vulnerabilities from the ENISA EU Vulnerability Database (EUVD, https://euvd.enisa.europa.eu) — the official EU repository established under NIS2. Each EUVD entry has its own id (EUVD-YYYY-NNNNN) and usually aliases one or more CVE ids; it is upserted into XVULNERABILITY.VULNERABILITY keyed on the CVE (or the EUVD id when CVE-less): existing CVEs are enriched in place with their EUVDId / EUVDUrl (and CVSS/EPSS/description filled when missing), and EUVD-only entries are added as new vulnerabilities. Live mode queries the public EUVD API (recent / critical / exploited / search, or a specific CVE / EUVD id); offline mode parses a saved EUVD JSON export. No authentication required; no DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to an EUVD JSON export to import (offline mode).

parameters.name: mode
parameters.help: Live feed: latest vulnerabilities, critical only, known-exploited, or a filtered search.

parameters.name: cve
parameters.help: Fetch a single CVE's EUVD entry (e.g. CVE-2024-3400).

parameters.name: id
parameters.help: Fetch a single EUVD entry by its id (e.g. EUVD-2024-45012).

parameters.name: query
parameters.help: mode=search: free-text query.

parameters.name: vendor
parameters.help: mode=search: filter by vendor name.

parameters.name: product
parameters.help: mode=search: filter by product name.

parameters.name: max
parameters.help: Maximum entries to import (paginated for search).

connectors.name: Google Threat Intelligence (GTIG/Mandiant) blog
connectors.description: Imports the latest posts from Google's Threat Intelligence blog (Google Threat Intelligence Group / Mandiant, https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/threat-intelligence) via its RSS feed (https://cloudblog.withgoogle.com/topics/threat-intelligence/rss/). Each post (title, summary, author, date) becomes an XTHREAT.INTELEXCHANGE item, idempotent by post URL; MITRE ATT&CK technique IDs, CVEs and adversary group codes (APT##, UNC####, FIN##, TEMP.*, UTA-###) found in the text are extracted as tags, and ATT&CK techniques are cross-linked into INTELEXCHANGEATTACK so they count toward the ATT&CK coverage. Read-only, non-intrusive, no authentication, Python stdlib only (no extra worker packages). Override the feed with the GCTI_FEED_URL worker env var. Offline import via the 'file' parameter (a saved RSS XML).

parameters.name: max_items
parameters.help: Maximum number of blog posts to import (most recent first).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Offline mode: parse a saved RSS XML file instead of fetching the live feed.

connectors.name: GLPI — IT Asset Management (CMDB)

connectors.name: GraphQL Cop — GraphQL API security audit
connectors.description: Runs GraphQL Cop (https://github.com/dolevf/graphql-cop) against a GraphQL endpoint and imports its findings. GraphQL Cop probes for introspection enabled, GraphiQL/Playground exposed, alias/field/directive/circular-query overloading (DoS), batch queries (DoS), GET-based & urlencoded POST queries (CSRF), and tracing/debug info-leak. The scanned endpoint becomes an ASSET; each positive finding becomes a VULNERABILITY (severity from HIGH/LOW/INFO, with impact + a reproducible curl). Offline: pass `file` = a graphql-cop JSON export (`graphql-cop -t <url> -o json`). Live (ACTIVE WEB SCANNING — authorized scope only): pass `target` = the GraphQL endpoint URL, with the `graphql-cop` binary on the worker PATH (or GRAPHQL_COP_BIN). Recorded as a DevSecOps DAST scan.

parameters.name: target
parameters.help: Live mode: the GraphQL endpoint URL to scan (e.g. https://app.example.com/graphql). Must be in the engagement scope.

parameters.name: header
parameters.help: Live mode: extra header(s) as JSON passed to graphql-cop -H (e.g. {"Authorization":"Bearer …"}).

parameters.name: proxy
parameters.help: Live mode: HTTP(S) proxy URL passed to graphql-cop -x.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Offline mode: a graphql-cop JSON export to import.

connectors.description: Imports AI-agent attack-evaluation results from HackAgent (https://github.com/AISecurityLab/hackagent), a security-testing toolkit (Python, Apache-2.0) that probes AI agents for prompt injection, jailbreaking, goal hijacking and tool misuse using AdvPrefix, AutoDAN-Turbo, PAIR, TAP, FlipAttack, BoN, h4rm3l, CipherChat, PAP and Baseline techniques. Import-only: export results to JSON and this connector maps each AI agent under test to an ASSET and every SUCCESSFUL attack to a VULNERABILITY / ASSETVULNERABILITY (ref = HACKAGENT-<category>-<hash>; severity by attack category — jailbreak/prompt-injection/goal-hijack/tool-misuse = high, others = medium). Defensive parser: accepts a results array or a {results|runs|attacks|evaluations:[...]} object; only successful/failed-defence attacks become findings. No secrets and no DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a HackAgent results export (JSON) to import.

parameters.name: agent
parameters.help: Default asset name for the AI agent under test when a result carries no agent/target name.

parameters.name: all
parameters.help: Import every attempt as a finding, not only the successful attacks (default: successful only).

connectors.name: httpx
connectors.description: Imports HTTP probe results from ProjectDiscovery httpx (https://github.com/projectdiscovery/httpx). Each probed host becomes an ASSET (hostname + resolved IP), each live URL a SERVICE, and detected technologies are recorded as CPE-like components. Offline mode: parse an httpx JSON-lines output (`httpx -json`). Live mode: if `target` is set and the `httpx` binary is on the worker PATH, runs `httpx -json -silent` against it. Run live only within an authorised engagement scope. No DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to an httpx JSON-lines output file (`httpx -json`) to import.

parameters.name: target
parameters.help: Live mode: a host/URL (or path to a hosts file) to probe with the local `httpx` binary.

connectors.name: Jaeles — web vulnerability scan (signatures)
connectors.description: Automated web-application vulnerability scanning with Jaeles signatures. Each matched signature becomes a finding (VULNERABILITY / ASSETVULNERABILITY) on the target asset; CVE ids referenced by a signature are used as the reference. Intrusive — run only against authorized targets (the engagement scope is enforced by the runner). Install on the worker: go install github.com/jaeles-project/jaeles@latest, then `jaeles config init` to fetch signatures.

parameters.name: target
parameters.help: Target URL within the authorized engagement scope (e.g. https://example.com).

connectors.name: Jira
connectors.description: Imports security issues from Atlassian Jira (Cloud or Server/Data Center) into XORCISM as findings. Each Jira issue becomes a VULNERABILITY (CVE referenced in summary/description if present, else JIRA-<key>; severity mapped from the issue priority); the affected asset is resolved from the issue's component, an asset-like label, or the `asset`/`project` fallback. Live mode queries the Jira REST API (`/rest/api/3/search` with JQL, paginated); offline mode parses a saved Jira search-results JSON. Secrets via worker env only: JIRA_URL (base), JIRA_USER (email, Cloud) + JIRA_TOKEN (API token / PAT). No DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a Jira search-results JSON export to import (offline mode).

parameters.name: project
parameters.help: Live mode: Jira project key to import (builds JQL `project = KEY AND statusCategory != Done`).

parameters.name: jql
parameters.help: Live mode: explicit JQL overriding `project` (e.g. `labels in (security, vulnerability) AND statusCategory != Done`).

parameters.name: max
parameters.help: Live mode: maximum issues to import (paginated).

parameters.name: asset
parameters.help: Fallback asset name when an issue has no component/asset label (default: the project key).

connectors.name: Lyra — CTI / OSINT digital-risk monitor
connectors.description: Defensive OSINT / digital-risk-protection monitoring with Lyra (CTI Monitor by K3lgy, https://github.com/K3lgy/lyra). Lyra scans ~10 open-web / OSINT sources (Have I Been Pwned, DeHashed, VirusTotal, URLScan.io, Shodan, GitHub, paste sites, Google dorks, Ransomware.live, crt.sh) for public mentions, leaked credentials and exposed infrastructure of an organisation, scores each finding (CRITIQUE/ÉLEVÉ/MOYEN/FAIBLE) and exports a JSON report. This connector maps that report to the XORCISM model: discovered hosts / subdomains / IPs become ASSETs, Shodan-exposed services become SERVICEs, and each finding becomes a redacted exposure (VULN) on the relevant asset (or the monitored organisation as a fallback). For defensive use on YOUR OWN organisation only. Read-only (non-intrusive): no DB access in run.py (worker-safe). API keys are read from the worker ENVIRONMENT, never the UI: LYRA_HIBP, LYRA_VIRUSTOTAL, LYRA_SHODAN, LYRA_URLSCAN, LYRA_DEHASHED, LYRA_DEHASHED_EMAIL. Lyra's location: LYRA_PATH (default C:\tools\lyra\lyra.py) or `lyra.py` on PATH; live mode needs Python 3.8+ and `pip install requests` on the worker. Offline mode: pass a saved lyra_report.json via the 'file' parameter (no network, no keys).

parameters.name: name
parameters.help: Organisation / company name under monitoring (labels findings; fallback asset). Required for live mode.

parameters.name: domains
parameters.help: Comma-separated domains you own (e.g. acme.com,acme.fr). Drives HIBP / crt.sh / Shodan / VirusTotal.

parameters.name: emails
parameters.help: Comma-separated organisation email addresses to check for breach exposure.

parameters.name: brands
parameters.help: Comma-separated brand / product names to search for public mentions.

parameters.name: keywords
parameters.help: Extra search keywords (registration numbers, internal code names…).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Offline mode: path on the worker to a saved Lyra JSON report (lyra_report.json) to import instead of running a live scan.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.help: Maximum number of findings to import.

connectors.name: MISP — Threat Intelligence Sharing
connectors.description: Pulls events from a MISP instance (open-source threat-intelligence sharing platform, https://www.misp-project.org) via its REST API (/events/restSearch) and imports each event into XTHREAT.INTELEXCHANGE — idempotent by event reference. MITRE ATT&CK techniques (from MISP galaxy clusters and tags), threat-actor / intrusion-set galaxies, malware/tool galaxies, CVE references and an IOC summary (IP / domain / URL / hash / email counts) are extracted as tags, and ATT&CK techniques are cross-linked into INTELEXCHANGEATTACK so they count toward the ATT&CK coverage. Read-only, non-intrusive, Python stdlib only (no PyMISP needed). Live mode needs the worker env vars MISP_URL + MISP_KEY (set MISP_VERIFY_SSL=0 for a self-signed instance, MISP_DAYS for the look-back window). Offline / air-gapped import is possible via the 'file' parameter (a saved /events/restSearch response, a single event export, or a JSON list of events).

parameters.name: max_items
parameters.help: Maximum number of MISP events to import (most recent first).

parameters.name: days
parameters.help: Live mode: look-back window (in days) for published events. Overrides MISP_DAYS.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Offline mode: import from a saved MISP JSON (a /events/restSearch response, a single /events/view export, or a JSON list of events) instead of querying live. Mainly for testing / air-gapped use.

connectors.name: n8n
connectors.description: Drives n8n (fair-code workflow automation, the open-source SOAR/glue) from XORCISM. Live mode POSTs a context payload (target / IOC / incident) to an n8n workflow's webhook (N8N_WEBHOOK_URL or the `webhook` param; optional N8N_API_KEY header) and ingests the JSON the workflow returns; offline mode parses a saved n8n execution-result JSON. Any indicators the workflow returns are imported as CTI (XTHREAT.INTELEXCHANGE); any findings are imported as ASSET/VULNERABILITY. Reuses the JoasASantos/n8n-CyberSecurity-Workflows catalogue (IOC enrichment, phishing triage, alert routing, vuln prioritization, recon…) — see the Purple/Blue/Red Team Operations automation-playbook templates. Worker-safe: stdlib only, secrets via env.

parameters.name: webhook
parameters.help: n8n workflow webhook URL to trigger (falls back to the N8N_WEBHOOK_URL worker env var).

parameters.name: workflow
parameters.help: Workflow name/id to invoke (passed in the payload; informational).

parameters.name: context
parameters.help: Context to pass to the workflow (e.g. an IOC, URL, host or incident id).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Offline mode: path to a saved n8n execution-result JSON to import (instead of triggering a webhook).

connectors.name: Naabu
connectors.description: Imports open-port results from ProjectDiscovery naabu (https://github.com/projectdiscovery/naabu). Each scanned host becomes an ASSET (host + IP) and each open port a SERVICE (host:port). Offline mode: parse a naabu JSON-lines output (`naabu -json`) or a plain `host:port` list. Live mode: if `target` is set and the `naabu` binary is on the worker PATH, runs `naabu -json` against it. Intrusive (active port scan) — run live only within an authorised engagement scope. No DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a naabu `-json` output file (or a plain host:port list) to import.

parameters.name: target
parameters.help: Live mode: a host/CIDR (or path to a hosts file) to scan with the local `naabu` binary.

connectors.name: Obserae
connectors.description: Imports network observability from Obserae (spartan-conseil/obserae) — a self-hosted NetFlow/IPFIX collector that reconstructs sessions and builds a cartography of hosts & services. Parses an Obserae YAML export (cartography + sessions) and turns it into network discovery & monitoring data around ASSET: each host becomes/updates an ASSET, each listening service becomes an ASSET↔SERVICE relationship (ASSETSERVICE: protocol/port/service), and each reconstructed flow becomes a NETWORKSESSION (protocol, source↔destination assets/IPs/ports, bytes/packets, first/last seen, state). Surfaced at /network-sessions — useful for asset discovery, monitoring and SOC investigation. Worker-safe (no DB, no network); accepts YAML or JSON.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to an Obserae cartography + sessions export (YAML or JSON). See the connector README for the expected schema.

parameters.name: default_protocol
parameters.help: Protocol assumed when a session/service omits it (default tcp).

connectors.name: Okta - sign-on / access policy rules (Zero Trust policy register)
connectors.description: Inbound connector: ingests Okta sign-on / authentication policy rules into XCOMPLIANCE.ZTPOLICY - the Zero Trust policy register (NIST SP 800-207 Policy Engine view) behind the /zero-trust Automation & Governance pillars. Normalizes each rule to subject x resource x conditions x grant controls (require factor -> MFA, DENY -> block) and state (active | inactive). Modes: `file` (an exported policy-rules JSON array), `live` (Okta /api/v1/policies + /rules with an org URL + API token), or `demo`. Read-only against Okta. Config: OKTA_URL + OKTA_TOKEN (or pass org / token). Idempotent by rule id.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path to an exported Okta policy-rules JSON (an array of rule objects). A demo sample is used if omitted and no token is set.

parameters.name: org
parameters.help: Okta org base URL, e.g. https://acme.okta.com (overrides OKTA_URL)

parameters.name: token
parameters.help: Okta API token (SSWS) (overrides OKTA_TOKEN) for live /api/v1/policies + /rules

parameters.name: type
parameters.help: Okta policy type to pull (OKTA_SIGN_ON | ACCESS_POLICY)

connectors.name: Okta - System Log sign-ins (UEBA / Zero Trust)
connectors.description: Inbound connector: ingests Okta System Log authentication events into XORCISM.IDENTITYSIGNIN - the continuous-verification telemetry behind the /zero-trust Identity pillar and the UEBA session-risk. Normalizes user, time, IP, location, device, MFA, result. Modes: `file` (an exported System Log JSON array), `live` (Okta /api/v1/logs with an org URL + API token), or `demo` (a built-in sample). Read-only against Okta. Config: OKTA_URL + OKTA_TOKEN (or pass `org` / `token`). Idempotent by Okta event uuid.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path to an exported Okta System Log (a JSON array of log events). A demo sample is used if omitted and no token is set.

parameters.name: org
parameters.help: Okta org base URL, e.g. https://acme.okta.com (overrides OKTA_URL)

parameters.name: token
parameters.help: Okta API token (SSWS) (overrides OKTA_TOKEN) for live /api/v1/logs

parameters.name: limit
parameters.help: Max events to pull in live mode

connectors.name: OpenCTI — Cyber Threat Intelligence
connectors.description: Pulls Reports from an OpenCTI platform (open cyber-threat-intelligence platform built on STIX 2.1, https://www.opencti.io) via its GraphQL API and imports each report into XTHREAT.INTELEXCHANGE — idempotent by report reference. The report's contained objects are resolved into tags: MITRE ATT&CK techniques (attack-pattern → x_mitre_id) are cross-linked into INTELEXCHANGEATTACK for ATT&CK coverage; intrusion-sets / threat actors, malware / tools and CVE vulnerabilities become actor / malware / CVE tags; report labels become free tags. Read-only, non-intrusive, Python stdlib only (no pycti SDK needed). Live mode needs the worker env vars OPENCTI_URL + OPENCTI_TOKEN. Offline / air-gapped import is possible via the 'file' parameter — either a STIX 2.1 bundle (a JSON {"objects": [...]} export, the universal format) or a saved OpenCTI GraphQL response.

parameters.name: max_items
parameters.help: Maximum number of OpenCTI reports to import (most recent first).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Offline mode: import from a STIX 2.1 bundle (JSON with an 'objects' array) or a saved OpenCTI GraphQL response, instead of querying live. Mainly for testing / air-gapped use.

connectors.name: OpenSCAP — SCAP/OVAL/XCCDF compliance & vulnerability
connectors.description: Imports an OpenSCAP (https://www.open-scap.org) report into the XORCISM model. Run `oscap` yourself and pass the output file: OVAL results (`oscap oval eval --results res.xml <oval.xml>`), XCCDF results (`oscap xccdf eval --results res.xml <ds.xml>`) or an ARF report (`--results-arf arf.xml`, which bundles both). The scanned host becomes an ASSET; OVAL vulnerability-class `true` definitions become VULN findings (the referenced CVE), OVAL inventory-class `true` become CPE (installed software), and failed XCCDF rules become VULN findings (compliance gaps). Read-only import (no DB access in run.py, worker-safe). This is the file-based counterpart of the XOR agent's live OVAL scan (/api/agent/oval).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to an oscap report: OVAL results, XCCDF results, or an ARF (.xml) report.

parameters.name: target
parameters.help: Asset name/host for the report (default: the report's host name, else the file name).

connectors.name: OpenVAS / Greenbone
connectors.description: OpenVAS / Greenbone Vulnerability Management (GVM). Offline mode parses a GMP XML report export (<report><results><result>…): each scanned host becomes an ASSET and each result becomes a VULNERABILITY (CVE refs, else NVT:<oid>; severity from the threat/CVSS). Live mode drives gvmd over GMP (python-gvm) — creates a target, runs a 'Full and fast' scan task, waits for completion, fetches the report and imports it; connection + credentials via worker env GVM_HOST/GVM_PORT (or GVM_SOCKET) + GVM_USER/GVM_PASSWORD. Active network vulnerability scanning (intrusive) in live mode. No DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a Greenbone GMP XML report to import (offline mode).

parameters.name: target
parameters.help: Live mode: host or IP/CIDR for gvmd to scan (must be in the engagement scope).

parameters.name: min_cvss
parameters.help: Minimum CVSS severity to import.

connectors.name: Opsgenie — alerts import
connectors.description: Imports alerts from Atlassian Opsgenie (on-call alerting & incident management) via the REST API into XORCISM as security alerts (XINCIDENT.ALERT). Each Opsgenie alert becomes an ALERT (idempotent by its id); priority P1-P5 maps to severity, tags and owner are carried over. Config (worker environment variables): OPSGENIE_API_KEY (API integration key); optional OPSGENIE_EU=1 for the EU region endpoint. Offline: pass `file` = a /v2/alerts export JSON, or run with no config for the bundled sample.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.help: Maximum number of alerts to import

parameters.name: query
parameters.help: Opsgenie search query, e.g. 'status: open AND priority < P3'

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Offline: a saved /v2/alerts export JSON to parse instead of the live API

connectors.name: OWASP AI Exchange — Agent Threat Advisor
connectors.description: Threat-models an AI / agentic system against the OWASP AI Exchange (https://owaspai.org). Describe the system (LLM app / autonomous agent / ML model; whether it uses tools, has memory, acts autonomously, ingests external content or handles sensitive data) and the advisor returns the applicable AI threats — prompt injection, excessive agency, tool misuse, memory poisoning, rogue agents, identity abuse, data poisoning, model theft and more — each with its lifecycle phase, impact and mitigating controls. Read-only advisory: no DB access, no network, no target (worker-safe). Mirrors the in-app /ai-threat-advisor.

parameters.name: system_type
parameters.help: Kind of AI system: llm (LLM application), agent (autonomous agent), ml (classic ML model).

parameters.name: uses_tools
parameters.help: The system can invoke tools / functions / actions.

parameters.name: has_memory
parameters.help: The system has persistent memory or a RAG store.

parameters.name: autonomous
parameters.help: The system acts autonomously (plans and executes without per-step approval).

parameters.name: external_data
parameters.help: The system ingests external / untrusted content (web, docs, emails, tool output).

parameters.name: sensitive_data
parameters.help: The system handles sensitive data (PII, secrets, regulated data).

connectors.name: Pentest Copilot
connectors.description: Imports findings from Pentest Copilot (https://github.com/bugbasesecurity/pentest-copilot), an AI-driven autonomous penetration-testing agent (TypeScript/Node, MIT) that drives 100+ security tools, Burp Suite and browser automation across an engagement. Import-only: export a session's findings to JSON and this connector maps each finding to an XORCISM finding — the affected target/host/URL becomes an ASSET and each issue a VULNERABILITY / ASSETVULNERABILITY (ref = CVE when referenced, else PCOPILOT-<hash>; severity from the finding's risk level). Defensive parser: accepts a findings array or a {findings|results|issues|vulnerabilities:[...]} object. No secrets and no DB access in run.py (worker-safe). NOTE: run Pentest Copilot itself only against systems you are authorized to test.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a Pentest Copilot findings export (JSON) to import.

parameters.name: target
parameters.help: Default asset name for findings whose record carries no target/host/URL.

connectors.name: PentesterFlow Agent
connectors.description: Imports confirmed findings produced by PentesterFlow Agent (https://github.com/PentesterFlow/agent), an agentic AI terminal assistant for authorized offensive security. The analyst runs the agent interactively (human-in-the-loop, scope-enforced) and it saves findings as Markdown in ./findings/ plus JSON-lines session logs. This connector parses those outputs: the engagement target becomes an ASSET (host); each confirmed finding (recon/web vuln/SSRF/SSTI/JWT/GraphQL/etc.) becomes a VULNERABILITY (CVE ref when present, else the finding title, with severity). Import-only — it does NOT run the autonomous agent or any LLM (the agent is driven by the analyst, by design). No DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

parameters.name: dir
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a PentesterFlow findings directory (e.g. ./findings) — every *.md finding (and *.json/*.jsonl session log) inside is imported.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path to a single finding (Markdown .md) or a JSON / JSON-lines session log to import.

parameters.name: target
parameters.help: Default engagement target (host or URL) for findings that don't carry their own target; must be within the engagement scope.

connectors.name: puredns
connectors.description: Fast domain resolver and subdomain brute-forcing tool (https://github.com/d3mondev/puredns). Wraps massdns to brute-force a domain against a wordlist (or resolve a list of candidate domains), filters DNS wildcards and validates answers against trusted resolvers — yielding the set of live, resolvable subdomains. Each resolved subdomain becomes an ASSET (host) on the target domain, expanding the known attack surface for the recon → web-scan attack chain. Offline mode: parse a puredns results file (one resolved domain per line, or a massdns `-o S` text export with `name. A ip`). Live mode: if `target` is set and the `puredns` binary is on the worker PATH, brute-force/resolve and parse. Active DNS enumeration (intrusive). No DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Offline mode: path on the worker to a puredns results file to parse (resolved subdomains, one per line, or a massdns `-o S` export). Use this when the binary/wordlist aren't on the worker.

parameters.name: target
parameters.help: Live mode: the apex domain to enumerate (e.g. example.com). Must be inside the engagement scope. The runner enforces scope on type 'target'.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.help: Live mode: 'bruteforce' brute-forces <wordlist> against <target>; 'resolve' resolves the candidate domains in <wordlist> (treated as a domains file).

parameters.name: wordlist
parameters.help: Live mode: path on the worker to the subdomain wordlist (bruteforce) or candidate-domains file (resolve). Required for live mode.

parameters.name: resolvers
parameters.help: Live mode: path on the worker to a trusted-resolvers file passed to puredns via -r (recommended; reduces false positives).

connectors.name: RedAmon
connectors.description: Imports findings from RedAmon (https://github.com/samugit83/redamon), an autonomous AI red-team framework (recon + exploitation + post-exploitation) that builds a Neo4j attack-surface graph and exports JSON scan results. This connector parses RedAmon's exports and maps them into XORCISM: discovered hosts / domains / subdomains / IPs become ASSETs; open ports / services / detected technologies become services & CPEs on those assets; vulnerability findings (Nuclei templates, GVM/OpenVAS NVTs, CVEs) become VULNERABILITYs (CVE/template ref + severity). Reads a single JSON/JSONL export ('file'), a directory of exports ('dir'), or pulls JSON from a RedAmon backend export URL ('endpoint', authenticated with the REDAMON_API_KEY worker env var). Defensive multi-shape parser (findings lists, Nuclei JSONL, host/port records, Neo4j node exports). Import-only — it does NOT run the RedAmon framework or any LLM. No XORCISM DB access in run.py (worker-safe). Secrets via worker env only (REDAMON_API_KEY), never in the manifest or UI.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a RedAmon JSON / JSON-lines export to import.

parameters.name: dir
parameters.help: Path to a directory of RedAmon exports — every *.json / *.jsonl inside is imported.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.help: Optional RedAmon backend export URL returning JSON findings; sent with 'Authorization: Bearer <REDAMON_API_KEY>' (worker env).

parameters.name: target
parameters.help: Default engagement target (host or URL) for findings that don't carry their own; must be within the engagement scope.

connectors.name: Rudder — nodes & compliance import
connectors.description: Imports managed nodes and configuration-compliance state from a Rudder server (https://www.rudder.io) via its REST API (X-API-Token). Each managed node becomes an ASSET (hostname, IP, OS); installed software (opt-in) becomes a CPE linked to the node; each rule a node is not fully compliant with becomes a finding (ref RUDDER-<ruleId>, severity from the compliance %). Read-only, non-intrusive. Configuration via worker environment variables (never entered in the UI): RUDDER_URL (e.g. https://rudder.example.com — the /rudder/api/latest path is appended automatically), RUDDER_API_TOKEN, RUDDER_VERIFY_TLS (set 0 to skip TLS verification for self-signed servers). Requires `pip install requests` on the worker.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.help: Import inventory only (nodes), compliance findings only, or both (all).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.help: Maximum number of nodes to import.

parameters.name: min_compliance
parameters.help: Flag a node↔rule as a finding when its compliance %% is below this threshold (default 100 = any rule not fully compliant).

parameters.name: include_software
parameters.help: Also import each node's installed software as CPEs (can be large).

connectors.name: Rustinel
connectors.description: Open-source cross-platform endpoint detection engine (EDR). Collects native host telemetry via ETW (Windows), eBPF (Linux) and Endpoint Security (macOS), normalizes it into a shared event model, and evaluates it against Sigma rules, YARA signatures and atomic IOCs — emitting ECS-compatible NDJSON alerts for SIEM pipelines. Tool: https://github.com/Karib0u/rustinel. This connector parses Rustinel's ECS NDJSON alert log (logs/alerts.json.<date>) and maps each detection to a finding on the originating host asset.

connectors.name: SailPoint
connectors.description: SailPoint Identity Security Cloud (formerly IdentityNow) identity-governance sync via the REST API. Collects governed identities (→ human identities, with lifecycle state → Status, manager and identity profile) and optionally source accounts (→ non-human / uncorrelated identities) into XORCISM (IDENTITY), so the IGA system of record feeds the Identity & IAM inventory for orphaned/uncorrelated-account, stale and lifecycle-state detection. App-only auth (OAuth2 client credentials) — secrets from the worker environment: SAILPOINT_BASE_URL, SAILPOINT_CLIENT_ID, SAILPOINT_CLIENT_SECRET. API: https://developer.sailpoint.com/docs/api/.

connectors.name: SARIF — import static-analysis results
connectors.description: Imports an OASIS SARIF 2.1.0 document (Static Analysis Results Interchange Format) produced by any compatible tool — CodeQL, Semgrep, Bandit, ESLint, Trivy, Grype, Checkov, gitleaks, nuclei -sarif, and more. The analyzed project/repository becomes an ASSET; each SARIF result becomes a finding (VULNERABILITY / ASSETVULNERABILITY) with severity from the SARIF level or the security-severity property, and the tool, rule, message and file:line in the description. A CVE referenced by a rule/result is used as the finding reference (otherwise a stable SARIF-<rule> ref is derived). Read-only file import, no secrets. Point 'file' at a .sarif/.json on the worker.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a SARIF (.sarif/.json) document to import.

parameters.name: project
parameters.help: Application/asset name to attach the findings to (default: derived from the SARIF repository URI / tool name).

connectors.description: Imports incidents / security incidents from ServiceNow (the market-leading ITSM platform) via the Table API into XORCISM as security alerts (XINCIDENT.ALERT). Each ServiceNow record becomes an ALERT (idempotent by its number), priority maps to severity, and the impacted CI is linked to its ASSET. Config (worker environment variables): SERVICENOW_INSTANCE (e.g. dev12345.service-now.com), SERVICENOW_USER, SERVICENOW_PASSWORD (basic auth); optional SERVICENOW_TABLE (default incident; use sn_si_incident for Security Incident Response). Offline: pass `file` = a Table-API export JSON, or run with no config for the bundled sample.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.help: Maximum number of records to import

parameters.name: query
parameters.help: ServiceNow encoded query (sysparm_query), e.g. active=true^priority<=2

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Offline: a saved Table-API export JSON to parse instead of the live API

connectors.name: Shodan
connectors.description: Imports internet-exposure data from Shodan (https://www.shodan.io). Each host becomes an ASSET (IP, OS), each banner an exposed SERVICE (host:port + product/version), and detected CPEs are linked to the asset. Offline mode: parse a `shodan host <ip>` / `shodan download`+`shodan parse` JSON (a single host object, a JSON-lines banner stream, or `{matches:[...]}`). Live mode: if `query` is set and SHODAN_API_KEY is in the worker env, calls the Shodan search API (needs `pip install requests`). Read-only OSINT (non-intrusive). No DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a Shodan JSON (host object, JSON-lines banners, or {matches:[...]}) to import.

parameters.name: query
parameters.help: Live mode: Shodan search query (needs SHODAN_API_KEY in the worker env).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.help: Live mode: maximum number of results to import.

connectors.name: SOC Prime — Detection content (Sigma)
connectors.description: Imports detection content (Sigma rules) from SOC Prime's Threat Detection Marketplace / Platform (https://socprime.com) — the world's largest library of curated, behavior-based detections. Each Sigma rule is imported into XTHREAT.SIGMARULE (idempotent by rule reference), where it boosts the Threat-Informed Defense 'detect' pillar and Purple-Team coverage; AND it is recorded as an XTHREAT.INTELEXCHANGE item so the detection shows in the intel feed and its MITRE ATT&CK techniques (from the rule's `tags: attack.tXXXX`) are cross-linked into INTELEXCHANGEATTACK for ATT&CK coverage. Read-only, non-intrusive, Python stdlib only (PyYAML used if installed, else a built-in Sigma field extractor). Live mode needs the worker env var SOCPRIME_API_KEY (and optional SOCPRIME_API_URL). Offline import is supported via the 'file'/'path' parameter — a single Sigma .yml file, a directory of .yml rules, or a JSON list/export of rules.

parameters.name: path
parameters.help: Offline mode: a Sigma rule file (.yml), a directory of Sigma rules, or a JSON list/export of rules to import. Worker-local.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Alias of 'path' (a saved SOC Prime / Sigma export JSON).

parameters.name: max_items
parameters.help: Maximum number of detection rules to import.

connectors.name: Taranis AI
connectors.description: Imports OSINT / threat intelligence from Taranis AI (https://github.com/taranis-ai/taranis-ai), an AI-powered Open-Source Intelligence tool (Python, EUPL-1.2) that collects unstructured news from web/RSS sources, enriches them with NLP/AI, and lets analysts refine them into structured reports. This connector turns Taranis news items / reports into XORCISM threat-intel items in XTHREAT.INTELEXCHANGE — extracting CVE, MITRE ATT&CK and threat-actor tags (idempotent by reference, with ATT&CK cross-links). Offline mode: import a Taranis JSON export (news items / reports) via `file`. Live mode: set env TARANIS_URL (the Taranis Core base URL) and TARANIS_API_KEY (Bearer/JWT) and the connector fetches recent news items from the REST API (best-effort across versions). No DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a Taranis AI JSON export (news items / reports) to import.

parameters.name: max_items
parameters.help: Live mode: maximum number of news items to fetch from the Taranis API.

connectors.name: Microsoft Teams — outbound alerts / notifications
connectors.description: Outbound (action) connector: posts a XORCISM alert / incident / message to a Microsoft Teams channel via an incoming webhook. Supports both Teams payloads — a modern Adaptive Card (Power Automate / Workflows webhooks) and the legacy MessageCard (Office 365 'Incoming Webhook' connector, *.webhook.office.com) — auto-detected from the URL. Severity colours the card; with no webhook (or dry_run) it returns the built payload without sending. The complement to the in-app Teams distribution (teams.ts), which fans alerts/notifications out automatically. Config: TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL (or pass `webhook`). Does NOT read/write the database.

parameters.name: text
parameters.help: Message body. Required unless title is given.

parameters.name: title
parameters.help: Optional card title / alert name

parameters.name: severity
parameters.help: critical | high | medium | low | info — colours the card

parameters.name: link
parameters.help: Optional absolute URL (deep-link to the alert in XORCISM)

parameters.name: format
parameters.help: auto | adaptivecard | messagecard

parameters.name: webhook
parameters.help: Teams incoming-webhook URL (overrides TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL)

parameters.name: dry_run
parameters.help: Build the card but do not send it (returns the payload)

connectors.name: Telegram OSINT (Dark Web Informer)
connectors.description: Imports Telegram OSINT investigations from telegram_osint (Dark Web Informer edition, https://github.com/DarkWebInformer/telegram_osint). The tool profiles Telegram users, channels and groups (Telethon) and exports a structured JSON file (./exports/*.json) for the user / chat / members / search modes. This connector ingests that JSON export and maps it into the XORCISM model: the investigated subject (user / channel / group) becomes an ASSET (tg:<handle|title>); shared / linked real domains become host ASSETs; and notable items become findings (VULN) — a scam/fake/restricted flag, a keyword-search hit (snippet truncated), and investigative pivots (other @handles, t.me links, external/social links). Privacy-aware: phone numbers and personal names are NOT imported as findings — only handles, links, domains, risk flags and truncated keyword snippets. Read-only import (no DB access in run.py, worker-safe). Live collection needs Telegram API credentials + Telethon + interactive auth on YOUR account, so this connector is import-only: run telegram_osint.py yourself (`--export json`) and pass the exported JSON via the 'file' parameter.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a telegram_osint JSON export (exports/<name>_<mode>_<ts>.json) — user / chat / members / search.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.help: Maximum number of findings to import (keyword hits / pivots / shared domains).

connectors.name: theHarvester
connectors.description: Imports OSINT recon results from theHarvester (https://github.com/laramies/theHarvester). Discovered hosts and IP addresses become ASSETs (hostname + IP). Offline mode: parse a theHarvester JSON output file (`theHarvester -f <name>` → <name>.json). Live mode: if `domain` is set and the `theHarvester` binary is on the worker PATH, runs `theHarvester -d <domain> -b <sources> -f`. Read-only OSINT (non-intrusive). No DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a theHarvester JSON output file to import.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.help: Live mode: domain to harvest with the local `theHarvester` binary.

parameters.name: sources
parameters.help: Live mode: theHarvester sources passed to -b (default: all).

connectors.name: TheHive — alerts / cases import
connectors.description: Imports alerts (and cases) from TheHive (the open-source SOAR / case-management platform) into XORCISM as security alerts (XINCIDENT.ALERT, the Defender-XDR-aligned layer that feeds incidents). Each TheHive alert/case becomes an ALERT (idempotent by source ref); impacted hosts are linked to their ASSET. Config (worker environment variables): THEHIVE_URL (e.g. https://thehive.lab), THEHIVE_API_KEY (Bearer). Offline: pass `file` = a TheHive alert-export JSON, or run with no config to import the bundled sample.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.help: Maximum number of alerts to import

parameters.name: kind
parameters.help: alert | case

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Offline: a saved TheHive export JSON to parse instead of the live API

connectors.name: ThePhishAnalyzer
connectors.description: Analyzes a phishing email (.eml / RFC822) — typically a DOCUMENT attached in XORCISM — and extracts its indicators of compromise. Parses headers and authentication results (SPF / DKIM / DMARC), the sender and reply-path, all URLs and the domains/IPs they resolve to, and the SHA-256 of each attachment. Each indicator is imported into the CTI store (XTHREAT.INTELEXCHANGE) tagged 'phishing', and the email itself is recorded as a phishing report. Offline mode parses a local .eml file (worker-safe, no DB, no network). Mirrors achrafhachimiac/ThePhishAnalyzer's email-intake workflow.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to the phishing email to analyze (.eml / RFC822). In XORCISM this is usually an exported DOCUMENT.

parameters.name: label
parameters.help: Label(s) to tag the extracted indicators with (comma-separated).

connectors.name: Upwind
connectors.description: Imports cloud-security findings from Upwind (https://www.upwind.io), a runtime-powered CNAPP (cloud-native application protection: vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, runtime threats, identity & posture). Each affected cloud resource / host / image becomes an ASSET and every finding a VULNERABILITY / ASSETVULNERABILITY (ref = CVE when present, else UPWIND-<hash>; severity from the finding's risk level). Offline mode: export findings to JSON and import via `file` (accepts a findings array or a {findings|vulnerabilities|issues|results|data:[...]} object). Live mode: set env UPWIND_API_URL + UPWIND_CLIENT_ID + UPWIND_CLIENT_SECRET (OAuth2 client-credentials, best-effort) to pull findings from the API. No DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

connectors.name: VICE — web-app security auditor (SAST + DAST)
connectors.description: Security audit with VICE (vice-security, https://github.com/Webba-Creative-Technologies/vice) — a black-box & white-box auditor for web applications. White-box: audits a project directory (source code, .env exposure, npm audit, Supabase RLS, SQLi/XSS). Black-box: crawls a URL, extracts secrets from JS bundles, tests login for brute-force / SQLi, scans ports. VICE emits SARIF, so this connector maps its findings into the XORCISM model via the shared SARIF parser: the audited project / scanned host becomes an ASSET and each finding becomes a VULN (ref = CVE if referenced, else VICE-<rule>@<file:line>; severity from the SARIF security-severity / level). Read-only import (worker-safe, no DB access in run.py). Offline mode: pass a saved VICE SARIF report via the 'file' parameter. Live mode runs VICE (Node ≥18 + `npm install` in the clone, or `npm install -g vice-security`); locate it via VICE_PATH (default C:\tools\vice\bin\vice.js) or `vice` on PATH; VICE_ACCEPT_TERMS=1 is set automatically.

parameters.name: source
parameters.help: White-box: path on the worker to a project directory to audit (e.g. /srv/app). Maps to `vice audit <source>`.

parameters.name: url
parameters.help: Black-box: a URL in scope to scan (e.g. https://app.example.com). Maps to `vice scan --url <url>`. Intrusive — engagement scope is enforced.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Offline: path on the worker to a saved VICE SARIF report to import instead of running a scan.

parameters.name: min_score
parameters.help: White-box audit: VICE --min-score threshold (0 imports all findings).

connectors.name: VoidAccess
connectors.description: Imports dark-web OSINT / threat intelligence from VoidAccess (https://github.com/KatrielMoses/voidaccess), a self-hosted dark-web OSINT platform (Python, MIT) — a free alternative to Recorded Future / DarkOwl / Flare. It runs autonomous investigations across Tor search engines and clearnet sources, extracts entities (CVEs, threat actors, domains, IPs, emails, hashes, crypto wallets, paste links) and builds relationship graphs. This connector turns a VoidAccess investigation/findings export (JSON) into XORCISM threat-intel items in XTHREAT.INTELEXCHANGE — each finding becomes an intel item with CVE / MITRE ATT&CK / threat-actor tags and an indicator summary (idempotent by reference, with ATT&CK cross-links). Import-only via `file` (its REST API *triggers* live dark-web crawls, which is intentionally NOT exposed here). VoidAccess can also export STIX 2.1 bundles and MISP JSON events, which you can import via the opencti / misp connectors. Privacy: emails / wallets / hashes are summarised as counts, not dumped verbatim. No DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a VoidAccess investigation/findings export (JSON) to import.

parameters.name: max_items
parameters.help: Maximum number of findings to import.

connectors.name: Wazuh
connectors.description: Imports endpoint inventory and vulnerability findings from Wazuh (https://wazuh.com), the open-source XDR/SIEM. Each Wazuh agent becomes an ASSET (name + IP + OS); syscollector packages become components/CPEs; vulnerability-detector findings become VULNERABILITIES (CVE + severity) attached to their agent. Offline mode: parse a saved Wazuh API JSON (an /agents or /vulnerability/{agent} response, shape {data:{affected_items:[...]}}, or a {agents,vulnerabilities,packages} bundle). Live mode: if `manager` (or WAZUH_API_URL) is set and WAZUH_API_USER/WAZUH_API_PASSWORD are in the worker env, authenticates (JWT) and pulls agents + per-agent vulnerabilities (needs `pip install requests`). Read-only API queries (non-intrusive). No DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a saved Wazuh API JSON (/agents, /vulnerability/{agent}, or a {agents,vulnerabilities,packages} bundle) to import.

parameters.name: manager
parameters.help: Live mode: Wazuh server API base URL, e.g. https://wazuh.example:55000 (else WAZUH_API_URL in the worker env). Credentials come from WAZUH_API_USER/WAZUH_API_PASSWORD.

parameters.name: agent
parameters.help: Optional: agent name to attribute vulnerabilities to when an offline vulnerability export lacks the agent context.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.help: Live mode: maximum number of agents to enrich with vulnerabilities/packages.

connectors.name: Zabbix — Infrastructure Monitoring
connectors.description: Imports the monitored estate and active problems from Zabbix (open-source infrastructure monitoring for networks, servers, cloud and applications) into XORCISM Asset Monitoring. Each Zabbix host becomes an ASSET (network discovery) plus a MONITORINGCHECK (server monitor, status up/down/paused from host availability); each active problem becomes a MONITORINGINCIDENT (severity-mapped). Config (worker environment variables): ZABBIX_URL (the Zabbix front-end base, e.g. https://zabbix.local), ZABBIX_TOKEN (an API token) or ZABBIX_USER + ZABBIX_PASSWORD. Offline: pass `file` = a Zabbix export JSON ({hosts, problems} or a raw host.get response), or run with no config for the bundled sample.

connectors.description: Imports tickets from Zammad (the popular open-source help-desk / ticketing system) via the REST API into XORCISM as security alerts (XINCIDENT.ALERT). Config (worker environment variables): ZAMMAD_URL (e.g. https://support.acme.io), ZAMMAD_TOKEN (HTTP token access). Offline: pass `file` = a /api/v1/tickets export JSON, or run with no config for the bundled sample.

connectors.name: ZettelForge
connectors.description: Agentic CTI memory (ZettelForge, https://github.com/rolandpg/zettelforge): extracts and organises threat intelligence from analyst notes and reports so institutional knowledge isn't lost. Each note/report becomes an XTHREAT.INTELEXCHANGE item (idempotent by content reference); ZettelForge's entity extraction surfaces CVEs, MITRE ATT&CK techniques (Txxxx), threat-actor groups with alias resolution (Fancy Bear / Sofacy / STRONTIUM -> APT28), malware/tools and IOCs (IPs, domains, URLs, hashes, emails) as tags, and ATT&CK techniques are cross-linked into INTELEXCHANGEATTACK so they count toward the ATT&CK coverage. When the 'zettelforge' Python package is installed (pip install zettelforge) it is used for LLM-grade NER and the notes are also persisted into ZettelForge's local knowledge graph (data dir ZETTELFORGE_DATA_DIR, default ~/.amem); otherwise a built-in regex+alias extractor (stdlib only, fully offline) is used. Read-only, non-intrusive, no external API keys. Point 'notes' at a file or a directory of markdown/text notes, or use 'file' to import a saved ZettelForge JSON export.

parameters.name: notes
parameters.help: Path to a CTI note/report file, or a directory of .md/.txt/.markdown notes (worker-local). Entities are extracted from each and imported into INTELEXCHANGE.

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Offline mode: import a saved ZettelForge JSON export (memories/entities) instead of extracting from notes.

parameters.name: author
parameters.help: Author/source recorded on the imported intel items.

parameters.name: max_items
parameters.help: Maximum number of notes/items to import.

parameters.name: use_zettelforge
parameters.help: Use the installed 'zettelforge' package for entity extraction + knowledge-graph persistence when available (falls back to the built-in regex extractor).

connectors.name: Zircolite
connectors.description: Imports detections from Zircolite (https://github.com/wagga40/Zircolite), a standalone SIGMA-based detection tool for logs (Windows EVTX/XML/JSONL, Auditd, Sysmon for Linux, CSV, JSON array). Each log source's Computer/host becomes an ASSET and every fired Sigma rule becomes a finding (VULNERABILITY / ASSETVULNERABILITY) with the rule level as severity and the ATT&CK technique(s) embedded in the finding name. Offline mode: import a Zircolite JSON report (`zircolite.py ... -o detected_events.json`). Live mode: if `events` and `ruleset` are set and Zircolite is on the worker (env ZIRCOLITE_PATH, default C:\tools\Zircolite\zircolite.py, else `zircolite` on PATH; optional env ZIRCOLITE_RULESET), runs `zircolite --events <events> --ruleset <ruleset> -o <out>` and parses the result. Non-intrusive local log triage. No secrets. No DB access in run.py (worker-safe).

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path on the worker to a Zircolite JSON report (`zircolite.py ... -o detected_events.json`) to import.

parameters.name: events
parameters.help: Live mode: path to the logs to scan (EVTX/XML/JSONL/Auditd/Sysmon/CSV/JSON file or folder).

parameters.name: ruleset
parameters.help: Live mode: path to the Sigma ruleset (JSON or YAML) Zircolite should use (or set env ZIRCOLITE_RULESET).

parameters.name: host
parameters.help: Default asset name for detections whose log events carry no Computer/host field.

connectors.name: Zero Trust posture push (ZTNA / IdP enforcement plane)
connectors.description: Outbound (action) connector: pushes the XORCISM device-trust feed (GET /api/zero-trust/device-trust) to your enforcement plane — a ZTNA / IdP / PEP device-posture endpoint (Cloudflare Access, Zscaler, Microsoft Entra Conditional Access, Okta or a custom PDP) — so access decisions can gate on the XORCISM-computed device trust. Reads the feed from a `file` (the exported JSON), an inline `feed`, or builds a demo feed; shapes the payload per target (generic | cloudflare | zscaler | okta | entra); only the devices below `min_trust` (the non-compliant set) are pushed. With no webhook (or dry_run) it returns the built payload WITHOUT sending. Config: ZT_POSTURE_WEBHOOK / ZT_POSTURE_TOKEN (or pass `webhook` / `token`). Does NOT read or write the database.

parameters.name: webhook
parameters.help: Enforcement-plane device-posture webhook URL (overrides ZT_POSTURE_WEBHOOK)

parameters.name: format
parameters.help: generic | cloudflare | zscaler | okta | entra — shapes the per-device payload

parameters.name: file
parameters.help: Path to the exported device-trust JSON (GET /api/zero-trust/device-trust). A demo feed is used if omitted.

parameters.name: feed
parameters.help: Inline device-trust JSON (alternative to file)

parameters.name: min_trust
parameters.help: Push only devices with trust below this threshold (the non-compliant set); 0 = push all

parameters.name: token
parameters.help: Bearer/API token for the webhook (overrides ZT_POSTURE_TOKEN)

parameters.name: dry_run
parameters.help: Build the payload but do not send it (returns the payload)
